cell_board: The image shows a rectangular board made of colored cells.
The image shows a colored grid board.
The image shows a cell board with one colored tile in each grid position.
The image shows a rectangular tile grid.
The image shows a board of colored square or rectangular cells.

cell_board_topology_query: Use side-adjacent cells for movement or connectedness; diagonal touching does not connect cells.
For any path, reachability, or component question, use only up, down, left, and right steps.
Treat cells sharing an edge as neighbors; cells meeting only at a corner are not neighbors.
Use the board cells exactly as drawn; only edge-sharing cells are adjacent.
When checking connected regions or movement, count orthogonal cell steps only.
cell_board_symmetry_query: Use the stated mirror axis and count only the requested side.
Compare each counted-side cell with its mirror cell across the stated axis.
Only cells on the named side contribute to the mismatch count.
Mirror matches require the two paired cells to have the same color.
Use the visible colored cells to check the stated mirror symmetry.

largest_component_size: Among the connected {query_color} groups, how many cells are in the largest group?
Find the size of the largest side-connected {query_color} component.
What is the largest number of cells in any connected {query_color} group?
How many cells does the biggest connected {query_color} region contain?
Count the cells in the largest connected group of {query_color} cells.
reachable_region_size: Dark cells are walls. The passable cells are the light cells plus the green S cell. Starting from S, how many passable cells are reachable by side-adjacent moves, including S?
From the green S cell, count every reachable passable cell, including S. Light cells are passable, dark wall cells block movement, and separated light cells do not count.
How many passable cells can be reached from the green S cell using only up/down/left/right moves? Include S, count light reachable cells, and do not count separated light cells.
Using side-adjacent moves from S, count the reachable passable cells. Dark cells are walls, light cells are passable, and S is included.
Count the reachable region from the green S cell, including the start cell S. Move only through light cells and do not cross dark walls.
shortest_path_length_value: Dark cells are walls and light cells are passable. What is the shortest path length from the green S cell to the red G cell?
Find the fewest side-adjacent steps from S to G through light passable cells; dark cells are walls.
How many orthogonal steps are in the shortest path from S to G without crossing dark wall cells?
What is the minimum number of cell moves needed to go from S to G through passable light cells?
Count the shortest side-step path length between S and G; dark cells cannot be used.
symmetry_violation_count: Using the {mirror_axis} mirror axis, how many cells on the {counted_side} side do not match their mirror cell?
Check {mirror_axis} symmetry. Count the mismatching cells on the {counted_side} side only.
How many {counted_side}-side cells violate {mirror_axis} mirror symmetry?
For the {mirror_axis} mirror check, count cells on the {counted_side} side whose mirror has a different color.
Count the {counted_side}-side cells that break {mirror_axis} symmetry.

query:largest_component_size.answer_hint: Set answer to the number of cells in the largest target-color component.
query:largest_component_size.annotation_hint: Set annotation to a bbox_set containing image-pixel cell boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] for the largest connected component.

query:reachable_region_size.answer_hint: Set answer to the number of passable cells reachable from S by side-adjacent moves, including S.
query:reachable_region_size.annotation_hint: Set annotation to a bbox_set containing image-pixel cell boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] for all reachable passable cells, including S.

query:shortest_path_length_value.answer_hint: Set answer to the shortest path length in orthogonal cell steps.
query:shortest_path_length_value.annotation_hint: Set annotation to a segment_set: one image-pixel segment [[x0, y0], [x1, y1]] for each adjacent step along a shortest S-to-G path.

query:symmetry_violation_count.answer_hint: Set answer to the number of counted-side cells that fail the mirror check.
query:symmetry_violation_count.annotation_hint: Set annotation to a segment_set: one image-pixel segment [[x0, y0], [x1, y1]] from each counted-side violating cell center to its mirror-cell center.
